description: CHG Trigger Rules from YODA_OC1_OC2_OPERATIONAL_BRIEF.md v1.0. Yoda monitors these and raises CHG+Telegram alert when triggered.

TRIGGER-01.name: OC2 arrival
TRIGGER-01.action: 10-step setup sequence: Ollama config, Gemma4 install, Tailscale mesh, OpenClaw config update, HA validation

additionalActions.name: qwen3.5:cloud reassessment
additionalActions.action: Re-benchmark qwen3.5:cloud on OC2. Latency failure (42.3s avg) driven by thinking mode on tool/coding tasks. Re-test with think:false on all 5 benchmark tasks. If avg latency <=20s -> approve for async/batch Tier 2. If still fails -> close out permanently.

additionalActions.name: Orchestrator MD version bump
additionalActions.action: Version bump SOUL.md + YODA_RULES.md + ORCHESTRATOR.md to reflect OC2 architecture, HA state, Gemma4:26b local inference.

TRIGGER-02.name: Both OC2 nodes live
TRIGGER-02.condition: OC2-A and OC2-B both online and validated
TRIGGER-02.detection: manual — validated after TRIGGER-01 complete for both nodes
TRIGGER-02.action: Activate HA architecture. Configure NAS shared state.

TRIGGER-03.name: Gemma4 validated on OC2
TRIGGER-03.condition: Gemma4:26b confirmed running on OC2 at acceptable performance
TRIGGER-03.action: Switch Shield/Lex/Sage/Warden from Haiku to Gemma4:26b local (Tier 1)

TRIGGER-04.name: OpenClaw security patch
TRIGGER-04.condition: New OpenClaw version released with security fix
TRIGGER-04.action: Critical CVE: update within 48h. High: within 7 days. Raise CHG. Freeze client onboarding if P1.
TRIGGER-04.details: v2026.5.12: Released 2026-05-14. SECURITY RELEASE: Broad security/provenance hardening pass across gateway, browser, Slack, node pairing, sandbox, and transcript paths. Key security fixes: (1) Windows sandbox USERPROFILE isolation (credential-bearing binds denied), (2) Provider config/auth hardening (CVE mitigation: env-var credential inference protection via structured SecretRefs), (3) macOS TLS trust enforcement for gateway certificates, (4) Media response body optimization. Bedrock/Slack/OpenShell/Vertex plugins externalized. 100+ stability fixes included.
TRIGGER-04.notes: FIRED 2026-05-15T20:00:13Z. Security release v2026.5.12 detected. Installed: 2026.5.5 → Available: 2026.5.12. HIGH PRIORITY (7-day window). Security hardening improvements including env-var credential inference mitigation and sandbox isolation enhancements. Telegram alert sent to Ken. CHG pending.

TRIGGER-05.name: Ollama Cloud PoC PASS
TRIGGER-05.condition: PoC Phase 5 returns PASS or PARTIAL PASS
TRIGGER-05.action: Implement 4-tier model strategy. Ken decision gate required before Phase 6.
TRIGGER-05.note: Full PoC PASS (CHG-0120). kimi-k2.6:cloud: Q=4.6/5, L=6.8s avg — implemented as Tier 2. glm-5.1: FAIL (thinking mode, 221s+). qwen3.5:cloud: FAIL (42.3s avg with /no_think). Phase 6 implemented. Monthly glm-5.1 no-think check cron active.
TRIGGER-05.qwen35Findings: Quality 4.6/5 OK. Latency 42.3s FAIL (/no_think helped text tasks, not code/tool). Reassessment queued for TRIGGER-01-A (OC2 arrival).

TRIGGER-06.name: OpenClaw v4.0 ships
TRIGGER-06.detection: automated — same daily release check cron as TRIGGER-04
TRIGGER-06.action: P3 gate assessment. Present Ken with native multi-agent vs CrewAI evaluation within 24h.

TRIGGER-07.name: First P2 client
TRIGGER-07.condition: First paying client onboarded to P2 platform
TRIGGER-07.action: Execute onboarding checklist. Run S1-S7 compliance audit. Ensure Tier 0/1 only for client data.

TRIGGER-08.name: Daily API cost threshold
TRIGGER-08.action: T1 ($60): alert Ken. T2 ($80): escalate. T3 ($100): pause and alert.

TRIGGER-09.name: Warden model drift
TRIGGER-09.detection: automated — Warden cron every 15min, escalation via warden-escalation-pending.json
TRIGGER-09.action: Yoda remediates within 1 heartbeat. Log CHG. Alert Ken.

TRIGGER-10.name: Business stream migration OC1 to OC2
TRIGGER-10.condition: OC2 live, Aria + business agents ready to migrate
TRIGGER-10.action: Migrate Aria + specialist agents from OC1 to OC2. Update routing. Validate.

TRIGGER-11.name: glm-5.1 no-think mode availability
TRIGGER-11.condition: Monthly check: has Ollama released a no-think/non-reasoning variant of glm-5.1:cloud?
TRIGGER-11.detection: automated — monthly cron checks Ollama model library
TRIGGER-11.action: If no-think mode confirmed available: re-run B2 (coding) and B4 (tool-use) benchmarks with it. If avg latency <=20s and quality >=3.5 -> raise CHG to add as Tier 2 alongside kimi-k2.6. If still fails -> skip month, check again next month.
TRIGGER-11.note: glm-5.1 PoC failure: 221s+ latency (thinking mode). Model quality potential is unknown. Worth revisiting if no-think mode ships.

TRIGGER-12.name: Agent allowlist sync
TRIGGER-12.condition: CI Cycle B decision recorded (new models approved/deprecated) OR model-policy.json tierStrategy updated (new models added/removed)
TRIGGER-12.detection: automated -- allowlist-detect.sh cron (every 30 min). Compares policy lastUpdated + ci-agent-state.json cycleHistory against allowlist-sync-state.json lastSyncAt.
TRIGGER-12.action: Run zsh scripts/allowlist-sync.sh --source <ci-cycle-b|strategy-update>. Log CHG. Alert Ken via Telegram with diff summary.
TRIGGER-12.notes: Idempotent -- safe to run on every strategy change. Script exits 0 if already current, 1 if changes applied.

TRIGGER-QBR.name: Quarterly QBR — Agent Fleet Review + Orchestrator MD version bump
TRIGGER-QBR.action: Run Agent Fleet Review (TKT-0130). Version bump SOUL.md + YODA_RULES.md + ORCHESTRATOR.md. Run Roadmap Refinement ceremony (TKT-0125). Review Cassian Andor (TKT-0109).

TRIGGER-13.name: Semantic Memory Store Go-Live
TRIGGER-13.description: OC2 stable + MinIO proven in prod for 2 sprints → activate TKT-0153 semantic memory store. Deprecates MEMORY_TICKETS.md + MEMORY_DECISIONS.md (Option B).
TRIGGER-13.gate: OC2 commissioned + MinIO in prod 2+ sprints
TRIGGER-13.detectionMethod: manual — Yoda flags when gate conditions met

TRIGGER-14.name: CLAUDE RESTORE — Anthropic API Key Re-provisioning
TRIGGER-14.condition: Ken issues CLAUDE RESTORE keyword or Anthropic API credits restored
TRIGGER-14.detection: manual — Ken signals readiness to exit interim period
TRIGGER-14.action: Prompt Ken to provide new Anthropic API key, then: (1) Store in Keychain as 'ainchors-anthropic-api-key', (2) Add auth.profiles.anthropic:default to openclaw.json, (3) Add anthropic model aliases to agents.defaults.models, (4) Update model-policy.json whitelist to include Anthropic models, (5) Run validate-fallback-chain.sh, (6) Restore agent primary models from claude-restore-config.json
TRIGGER-14.notes: Created during CHG-0393 cleanup. All Anthropic config removed during interim period. Re-provisioning required before agents can use Claude models again.

TRIGGER-14.name: Phase 3: Event Sourcing Design
TRIGGER-14.condition: P2 stable — defined as: P2 launched, at least 2 SME clients live, platform uptime >99% for 30-day rolling window
TRIGGER-14.action: Initiate Phase 3 event sourcing design. Raise TKT for Atlas to design event sourcing migration from optimistic locking. Preserve TKT-0104 agent_state_history schema as seed.

TRIGGER-15.name: P1→P2 Checkpoint: Golden Blueprint Review
TRIGGER-15.condition: P2 launches — first SME client onboarded (target Aug 2026)
TRIGGER-15.action: Atlas: (1) refresh Nexus-System-Architecture-v1.0.md — full delta review, update Section 9 gap map, update agent roster, confirm locked decisions. (2) Revise Aevlith-Technology-Strategy-Roadmap-v1.0-Internal.md — update current state to P2 reality, revise P3/P4 sections. (3) Produce Architecture Delta Summary for Ken. (4) Deliver via webchat + Telegram. Ken replies APPROVED before P2 ops begin.

TRIGGER-16.name: P2→P4 Checkpoint: Golden Blueprint Review
TRIGGER-16.condition: TRIGGER-14 fires (post-P2 stable, event sourcing design begins)
TRIGGER-16.action: Atlas: (1) Full refresh of both golden blueprint documents. (2) Add Phase 3 event sourcing architecture to System Architecture doc. (3) Update strategy doc with FSI trajectory detail. (4) Architecture Delta Summary for Ken review.

TRIGGER-17.name: Annual Review: Golden Blueprints (May each year)
TRIGGER-17.action: Atlas: (1) Full review of both golden blueprints against live platform state. (2) Prune outdated content. (3) Revalidate OKRs and KRIs. (4) Confirm locked decisions still hold. (5) Update current state sections. (6) Ken approval before next year.

TRIGGER-18.name: Spark: LinkedIn stability arc post — Gateway event loop investigation complete
TRIGGER-18.condition: TKT-0177 closed (Telegram startup hang + memory leak root causes resolved)
TRIGGER-18.action: Brief Spark to write a LinkedIn post/article under the AIOps authority arc. Topic: building a production agentic AI platform — gateway stability, event loop saturation, how we diagnosed and fixed it. External-facing, no internal refs. Key angles: (1) what event loop saturation looks like in production AI platforms, (2) the diagnostic process (logs, P99 vs MAX delay), (3) the fix (nightly restart as mitigation, root cause investigation), (4) lessons for teams building always-on AI operations. Must be genuinely educational for AI practitioners/CTOs. No vanity metrics. Length: substantial post or short article format.

CHG-0331.title: POL-011 Skill Installation and Security Review Policy — formal policy production
CHG-0331.description: Produced POL-011 as part of TKT-0137 AC2 batch. Derived from approved Skill-Installation-Policy-v1.0.md. Status: APPROVED. Effective 2026-05-15. Owner: Yoda + Shield. Saved to docs/POL-011-Skill-Installation-Policy.md. Uploaded to Google Drive (Docs folder). policy-register.json created.

TRIGGER-03.description: OC2 Gemma4 swap-out: Replace Haiku (T1 governance) with gemma4:26b local. Validate gemma4:31b-coding-mtp-bf16 as T1 candidate for OC2 48GB.
actions: 1. Benchmark gemma4:26b on OC2 vs Haiku accuracy
2. Test gemma4:31b-coding-mtp-bf16 local inference on OC2 (MTP-BF16 variant)
3. Validate 2x speed claim from Ollama May 8 email
4. If pass rate >=75%, swap Haiku → gemma4 for T1 governance agents
TRIGGER-03.note: ENHANCED 2026-05-17: Added gemma4:31b-coding-mtp-bf16 per Ollama May 8 email (2x speed, MTP support on macOS MLX). OC2 48GB has headroom for BF16. Evaluate alongside :26b.
